sections.heading: Surgery
sections.content:
Surgical resection remains the primary curative treatment for localized RCC and a palliative option for select patients with metastatic disease. The goal is complete tumor removal while maximizing preservation of renal function when feasible.

Principles:
Nephron-sparing surgery (partial nephrectomy) is preferred for stage I tumors and whenever technically feasible to preserve renal function and reduce chronic kidney disease risk. [KID-A]
Radical nephrectomy is indicated for larger or locally advanced tumors (stage II, III, resectable T4) or when partial nephrectomy is not feasible. [KID-A, KID-2, KID-3]
Open, laparoscopic, or robotic techniques are acceptable; outcomes are equivalent in experienced hands. [KID-A]
Regional lymph node dissection is optional but should be considered for patients with adenopathy on imaging or palpable nodes at surgery. Extended dissection templates are provided for right- and left-sided tumors. [KID-A]
Ipsilateral adrenalectomy may be omitted if the adrenal gland is uninvolved and not high-risk. [KID-A]
Percutaneous ablation (cryosurgery, radiofrequency, microwave) is an option for clinical stage T1 lesions, particularly for non-surgical candidates. Biopsy prior to or at ablation is recommended. [KID-A, KID-1]
Stereotactic body radiation therapy (SBRT) is an option for non-optimal surgical candidates with T1 and T2a tumors. Referral to a high-volume center is recommended. [KID-A, KID-B]

Resectability criteria:
Resectability is determined by imaging and surgical assessment. For primary tumors, complete resection (R0) should be achievable. For metastatic disease, resectability of both primary and metastatic sites is considered. Key factors include tumor size, location, involvement of adjacent structures, and presence of distant metastases. For patients with clinical T2, T3, or T4 tumors without metastases but in whom complete resection is not feasible upfront, presurgical neoadjuvant therapy may be considered following confirmation of clear cell histology to achieve cytoreduction. [KID-2, KID-3]

Pre-operative optimisation:
Medical optimization (e.g., control of hypertension, diabetes, renal function assessment) and nutritional support. For hereditary syndromes, preoperative screening for pheochromocytoma (in VHL and PGL/PCC syndromes) is mandatory. [HERED-RCC-C]

cards.name: Partial Nephrectomy (Nephron-Sparing Surgery)
cards.detail:
approach: Open, laparoscopic, or robotic.
indications: Stage I–III tumors when technically feasible; imperative indications (solitary kidney, chronic kidney disease, bilateral tumors); hereditary RCC syndromes. [KID-A]
key_complications: Hemorrhage, urinary leak, renal function impairment, infection.
operative_mortality: Not specified in source; generally low in high-volume centers.

cards.name: Radical Nephrectomy
cards.detail:
approach: Open, laparoscopic, or robotic. Includes perifascial resection of kidney, perirenal fat, and regional lymph nodes.
indications: Stage II, III, resectable stage IV (T4, M0) disease; tumor extends into inferior vena cava or involves >50% of kidney; partial nephrectomy would result in poorly functioning kidney. [KID-A, KID-2, KID-3]
key_complications: Hemorrhage, injury to adjacent organs, chronic kidney disease, cardiovascular morbidity.
operative_mortality: Can reach 10% for complex cases with caval thrombus. [KID-A]

cards.name: Cytoreductive Nephrectomy
cards.detail:
approach: Open or minimally invasive.
indications: Stage IV, M1 disease with surgically resectable primary tumor in select patients (excellent PS, no brain metastasis). [KID-3]
key_complications: Same as radical nephrectomy.
operative_mortality: Not specified; patient selection is critical.

cards.name: Metastasectomy
cards.detail:
approach: Varies by site (e.g., thoracotomy for lung, craniotomy for brain).
indications: Oligometastatic disease with controlled primary tumor and good performance status. [KID-4]
key_complications: Site-specific; bleeding, infection, organ dysfunction.
operative_mortality: Low in selected patients.

cards.name: Percutaneous Ablation
cards.detail:
approach: Image-guided (CT or ultrasound).
indications: Clinical stage T1 renal lesions, particularly for non-surgical candidates. Suitable for masses ≤3 cm. [KID-A, KID-1]
key_complications: Pain, bleeding, infection, thermal injury to adjacent structures, incomplete ablation requiring retreatment.
operative_mortality: Very low.
